cards.back: Moving Average of the squared gradient

cards.front: What Adam algorithm does differently from RMSprop
cards.back: Adam adapts the learning rate for each parameter based on its magnitude, RMSprop adapts the learning rate based on the magnitude of the past gradients

cards.front: What is the primary goal of the Adam algorithm?
cards.back: To adapt the learning rate for each parameter based on its previous gradient and magnitude

cards.front: Key difference between RMSprop and Adam
cards.back: RMSprop uses a fixed decay rate for all parameters, Adam uses a learning rate that adapts to each parameter's magnitude
When the gradients are sparse or have a large magnitude, RMSprop can help prevent exploding gradients
